description_en: The epicenter of Seoul youth culture. Home to Hongik University, indie music venues, street performers, and clubs that run until dawn.
Seoul's Brooklyn. Former shoe-manufacturing district transformed into a hub of artisan cafes, concept stores, and pop-up events in converted warehouses.
600-year-old hanok village. Visiting hours restricted to 10am-5pm to protect residents. The most photogenic traditional neighborhood in Seoul with 6.4M annual visitors.
Classic art and antiques strip with calligraphy shops, ceramics, old-school tea houses. Connected to Ikseon-dong for a blend of tradition and trendy.
Seoul's most international neighborhood. Global cuisines, diverse nightlife, and cultural fusion near the former US army base.
The famous district of Gangnam Style. Korea's most affluent area with high-end shopping, K-beauty flagships, and a thriving business scene.
Seoul's answer to Tokyo's Golden Gai. Retro bars in industrial alleys where photographers and artists gather. The new-tro capital of Korea.
Quiet, walkable neighborhood along the Gyeongui Line Forest Park. Dense with independent cafes and local restaurants. Perfect for slow-paced exploration.
Seoul's busiest shopping district. Packed with K-beauty brands, street food stalls, and international tourists. The commercial heart of the city.
